panels.description: Total AI code review events logged in the selected repositories and time window.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: Total AI Reviews

panels.description: Percentage of AI reviews that received at least one 👍 or 👎 reaction from a developer. Low values mean developers are reading but not responding — high values show active engagement.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN reactions_total_count > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS value FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: Developer Reaction Rate

panels.description: Of all AI-reviewed PRs that were either merged or closed, what percentage made it through? Baseline for PRs without AI review: ~71.9%.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN pr.status = 'MERGED' THEN pr.id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 1) AS value FROM pull_requests pr INNER JOIN ai_reviews ar ON ar.pull_request_id = pr.id WHERE pr.status IN ('MERGED', 'CLOSED') AND ar.repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ar.ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(ar.created_date)

panels.title: AI-Reviewed PR Merge Rate

panels.description: Total 👍 reactions left by developers on AI review comments. A positive signal that developers found the feedback useful.

targets.rawSql: SELECT SUM(reactions_thumbs_up) AS value FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: 👍 Developer Thumbs Up

panels.description: Total 👎 reactions — developer signals that the AI review was unhelpful or wrong. Rising counts warrant a look at what the AI is flagging.

targets.rawSql: SELECT SUM(reactions_thumbs_down) AS value FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: 👎 Developer Thumbs Down

panels.description: Number of distinct repositories covered by AI code review in the current filter selection.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT repo_id) AS value FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: Repos with AI Review Coverage

panels.description: How risky is the current mix of pull requests? High-risk PRs are more likely to have CI failures and lower merge rates.

targets.rawSql: SELECT risk_level, COUNT(*) as count FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date) GROUP BY risk_level

panels.title: Risk Level Distribution

panels.description: Which AI tools are providing reviews in the selected repositories? Useful for checking tool adoption and mix.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ai_tool, COUNT(*) as count FROM ai_reviews WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring}) AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date) GROUP BY ai_tool

panels.title: Reviews by AI Tool

panels.description: AI reviews where CodeRabbit skipped the review due to label configuration (e.g. do-not-merge/work-in-progress). These PRs are excluded from all other panels on this dashboard.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS `Skipped Reviews`,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM _tool_aireview_reviews ar2
    JOIN project_mapping pm2 ON ar2.repo_id = pm2.row_id AND pm2.`table` = 'repos'
    WHERE pm2.project_name = '${project}'
      AND ar2.repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
      AND ar2.ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
      AND $__timeFilter(ar2.created_date)), 0), 1) AS `Skipped %`
FROM _tool_aireview_reviews ar
JOIN project_mapping pm ON ar.repo_id = pm.row_id AND pm.`table` = 'repos'
WHERE pm.project_name = '${project}'
  AND ar.repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ar.ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND ar.body LIKE '%Review skipped%'
  AND $__timeFilter(ar.created_date)

panels.title: Skipped AI Reviews
Review Iterations — How Often Did Developers Push Back?

panels.description: Distinct PRs that received at least one AI review in the selected time range. Each PR may have multiple review rounds — use this as the true denominator, not Total AI Reviews.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pull_request_id) AS value
FROM ai_reviews
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)

panels.title: Total PRs with AI Review

panels.description: PRs where the developer made no additional commit after the initial AI review — the AI reviewed once and was never re-triggered. This does not mean the review was ignored; the developer may have merged, fixed locally, or addressed issues without pushing again.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM (
  SELECT pull_request_id
  FROM ai_reviews
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
    AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)
  GROUP BY pull_request_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
) t

panels.title: PRs with No AI Review Rounds

panels.description: A PR with more than one AI review means the developer pushed new commits after the first review — the AI re-reviewed each time. This is the strongest proxy we have for developer engagement driven by AI feedback.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM (
  SELECT pull_request_id
  FROM ai_reviews
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
    AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)
  GROUP BY pull_request_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) t

panels.title: PRs with Multiple AI Review Rounds

panels.description: Average number of AI review rounds on PRs that went through more than one round. Each round represents a developer push followed by a new AI review.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(review_count), 1) AS value
FROM (
  SELECT pull_request_id, COUNT(*) AS review_count
  FROM ai_reviews
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
    AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)
  GROUP BY pull_request_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) t

panels.title: Avg Rounds per Iterated PR

panels.description: Human reviews (APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED) submitted after the first AI review on the same PR. This is a proxy for AI-triggered developer engagement — the human may have reviewed in response to the AI's feedback. Correlation only, not causation.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM _tool_github_pull_request_reviews hrv
JOIN (
  SELECT
    CAST(SUBSTRING_INDEX(ar.pull_request_id, ':', -1) AS UNSIGNED) AS pr_numeric_id,
    MIN(ar.created_date) AS first_ai_review
  FROM ai_reviews ar
  WHERE ar.project_name = '${project}' AND ar.repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
    AND ar.ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(ar.created_date)
  GROUP BY ar.pull_request_id
) ai ON hrv.pull_request_id = ai.pr_numeric_id
WHERE hrv.github_submit_at > ai.first_ai_review
  AND hrv.author_username NOT LIKE '%[bot]%'
  AND hrv.author_username NOT IN ('coderabbitai', 'qodo-merge-pro')
  AND hrv.state IN ('APPROVED', 'CHANGES_REQUESTED')

panels.title: Human Reviews After AI Review (proxy)

panels.description: "1 round (no re-review)" = the PR triggered exactly one AI review and was not reviewed again. This is the large majority — the developer may have merged, fixed without pushing again, or ignored the review. These PRs have no iteration signal.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN review_count = 1 THEN '1 round'
    WHEN review_count BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN '2–3 rounds'
    WHEN review_count BETWEEN 4 AND 5 THEN '4–5 rounds'
    WHEN review_count BETWEEN 6 AND 10 THEN '6–10 rounds'
    ELSE '11+ rounds'
  END AS `Review Rounds`,
  COUNT(*) AS `PRs`
FROM (
  SELECT pull_request_id, COUNT(*) AS review_count
  FROM ai_reviews
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
    AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(created_date)
  GROUP BY pull_request_id
) t
GROUP BY `Review Rounds`
ORDER BY MIN(review_count)

panels.title: Distribution of AI Review Rounds per PR

options.content:
## How Well Does AI Predict CI Failures?

This dashboard answers a simple question: **when the AI code reviewer says a pull request is risky, does CI actually fail?** And the reverse — when the AI says everything looks fine, does CI agree?

We look at PRs that have both an AI review and OCP CI test results. Flaky tests (tests that fail randomly regardless of the code change) are excluded so we only count real failures.

**What we're aiming for:**
- The AI should correctly warn about at least **60% of PRs that will fail CI** — and rank failing PRs higher than passing ones 80% of the time
- Both scores are currently shown as 0–1 (green = on target, red = needs improvement)

Use the **Repository** and **AI Tool** dropdowns above to filter to a specific codebase or tool.

panels.title: AI Warnings vs What Actually Happened

panels.description: The AI flagged this PR as risky (at the selected warning threshold) and CI tests confirmed a failure. A true positive — the AI was right.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND risk_score >= ${warning_threshold}
  AND had_ci_failure = 1

panels.title: AI Warned → CI Failed ✓ (TP)

panels.description: The AI flagged this PR as risky but CI tests passed. A false alarm — the AI was too cautious.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND risk_score >= ${warning_threshold}
  AND had_ci_failure = 0

panels.title: AI Warned → CI Passed — False Alarm (FP)

panels.description: The AI gave this PR a clean bill of health, but CI tests failed. A missed failure — the AI was too lenient.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND risk_score < ${warning_threshold}
  AND had_ci_failure = 1

panels.title: AI Missed → CI Failed ✗ (FN)

panels.description: The AI flagged this PR as safe and CI tests passed. A true negative — everything agreed.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND risk_score < ${warning_threshold}
  AND had_ci_failure = 0

panels.title: AI OK → CI Passed ✓ (TN)
How Good Are the AI Predictions?

panels.description:
Formula: Precision = TP / (TP + FP)

Of every PR the AI flagged as risky, how many actually had CI failures? High precision means warnings are trustworthy — developers don't waste time on false alarms.

options.noValue: n/a — AI made no warnings in this period

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
)
SELECT ROUND(tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(tp + fp, 0), 1) AS value FROM counts

panels.title: When AI Warns — How Often Is It Right?

panels.description:
Formula: Recall = TP / (TP + FN)

Of every PR that actually broke CI, how many did the AI flag in advance? High recall means fewer failures slip through undetected.

options.noValue: n/a — no CI failures matched in this period

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
)
SELECT ROUND(tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(tp + fn, 0), 1) AS value FROM counts

panels.title: How Many Real Failures Did AI Catch?

panels.description:
Formula: F1 = 2·TP / (2·TP + FP + FN)

The harmonic mean of Precision and Recall — balances both in a single number. Useful when you care equally about false alarms and missed failures. 1.0 = perfect, 0.0 = completely wrong.

options.noValue: n/a — no warnings or failures in this period

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
)
SELECT ROUND(2 * tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(2 * tp + fp + fn, 0), 1) AS value FROM counts

panels.title: Overall Prediction Balance (F1)

panels.description:
Formula: Specificity = TN / (TN + FP)

Of every PR that CI confirmed was safe, how many did the AI correctly leave alone? High specificity means developers working on low-risk PRs aren't being bothered with unnecessary warnings.

options.noValue: n/a — no AI-reviewed PRs with CI results in this period

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
)
SELECT ROUND(tn * 100.0 / NULLIF(tn + fp, 0), 1) AS value FROM counts

panels.title: How Often Does AI Correctly Clear Safe PRs?
Overall Prediction Quality — Single Scores That Don't Depend on Any Threshold

panels.description:
Formula: PR-AUC = area under the Precision-Recall curve across all risk thresholds

Summarises how well the AI balances catching real failures vs avoiding false alarms — across all three risk levels at once. 1.0 = perfect, 0.5 = no better than random. Target: ≥ 0.6.

Unlike a single threshold metric, this score doesn't change when you adjust the warning threshold above.

options.noValue: n/a — need both AI warnings and CI failures to compute

targets.rawSql:
WITH pr_data AS (
  SELECT risk_score AS max_risk_score, had_ci_failure AS ci_failed
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
),
thresholds AS (SELECT 0 AS t UNION ALL SELECT 10 UNION ALL SELECT 20 UNION ALL SELECT 50 UNION ALL SELECT 80 UNION ALL SELECT 100),
curve AS (
  SELECT th.t AS threshold,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn
  FROM thresholds th CROSS JOIN pr_data pd GROUP BY th.t
),
pr_points AS (
  SELECT threshold,
    COALESCE(tp * 1.0 / NULLIF(tp + fp, 0), 1.0) AS prec,
    COALESCE(tp * 1.0 / NULLIF(tp + fn, 0), 0.0) AS rec
  FROM curve
),
lag_points AS (
  SELECT threshold, prec, rec,
    LAG(prec) OVER (ORDER BY threshold ASC) AS prev_prec,
    LAG(rec)  OVER (ORDER BY threshold ASC) AS prev_rec
  FROM pr_points
)
SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN prev_rec IS NOT NULL AND prev_rec > rec
  THEN (prev_rec - rec) * (prec + prev_prec) / 2 ELSE 0 END), 3) AS value
FROM lag_points

panels.title: Warning Quality Score (target: 0.6+)

panels.description:
Formula: ROC-AUC = area under the ROC curve (TPR vs FPR) across all risk thresholds

Measures how well the AI separates failing PRs from safe ones regardless of where you draw the line. 1.0 = perfect separation, 0.5 = random. Target: ≥ 0.8.

Unlike a single threshold metric, this score doesn't change when you adjust the warning threshold above.

options.noValue: n/a — need both AI warnings and CI failures to compute

targets.rawSql:
WITH pr_data AS (
  SELECT risk_score AS max_risk_score, had_ci_failure AS ci_failed
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
),
thresholds AS (SELECT 0 AS t UNION ALL SELECT 10 UNION ALL SELECT 20 UNION ALL SELECT 50 UNION ALL SELECT 80 UNION ALL SELECT 100),
curve AS (
  SELECT th.t AS threshold,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  th.t AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn
  FROM thresholds th CROSS JOIN pr_data pd GROUP BY th.t
),
roc_points AS (
  SELECT threshold,
    COALESCE(fp * 1.0 / NULLIF(fp + tn, 0), 0.0) AS fpr,
    COALESCE(tp * 1.0 / NULLIF(tp + fn, 0), 0.0) AS tpr
  FROM curve
),
lag_points AS (
  SELECT threshold, fpr, tpr,
    LAG(fpr) OVER (ORDER BY threshold DESC) AS prev_fpr,
    LAG(tpr) OVER (ORDER BY threshold DESC) AS prev_tpr
  FROM roc_points
)
SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN prev_fpr IS NOT NULL AND fpr > prev_fpr
  THEN (fpr - prev_fpr) * (tpr + prev_tpr) / 2 ELSE 0 END), 3) AS value
FROM lag_points

panels.title: Risk Ranking Quality (target: 0.8+)

panels.description:
Formula: False Positive Rate (FPR) = FP / (FP + TN)

Of all PRs that CI confirmed safe, what percentage did the AI flag as risky anyway? Lower is better — high values mean developers are distracted by warnings that don't matter.

⚠️ Interpret with caution: this metric is sensitive to the warning threshold selected above and to the CI failure rate in the data. With only a few days of CI data where ~80% of matched PRs had failures, the denominator (FP + TN) is very small, making this number volatile. It will stabilise as more CI data accumulates.

options.noValue: n/a — no AI-reviewed PRs with CI results in this period

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
)
SELECT ROUND(fp * 100.0 / NULLIF(fp + tn, 0), 1) AS value FROM counts

panels.title: How Often Does AI Cry Wolf?

panels.description: Prediction quality metrics broken down by repository. Only repos where AI reviews and CI data overlap are shown. Repos with no matching CI data are excluded.

targets.rawSql:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    repo_id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score >= ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn,
    SUM(CASE WHEN risk_score < ${warning_threshold} AND had_ci_failure = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  GROUP BY repo_id
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(r.name, c.repo_id) AS Repository,
  c.tp + c.fp + c.fn + c.tn AS `PRs with CI`,
  c.tp AS `AI Warned → Failed (TP)`,
  c.fp AS `AI Warned → Passed (FP)`,
  c.fn AS `AI Missed → Failed (FN)`,
  c.tn AS `AI OK → Passed (TN)`,
  ROUND(100.0 * c.tp / NULLIF(c.tp + c.fp, 0), 1) AS `Precision %`,
  ROUND(100.0 * c.tp / NULLIF(c.tp + c.fn, 0), 1) AS `Recall %`,
  ROUND(100.0 * 2 * c.tp / NULLIF(2 * c.tp + c.fp + c.fn, 0), 1) AS `F1 %`
FROM counts c
LEFT JOIN repos r ON c.repo_id = r.id
HAVING c.tp + c.fp + c.fn + c.tn > 0
ORDER BY `PRs with CI` DESC

panels.title: Per-Repository Breakdown: AI Warnings vs CI Failures
What If We Used a Different Risk Level as the Warning Threshold?

options.content:
Right now, the AI flags a PR as risky when its risk score is 70 or above. But what if we raised or lowered that bar?

- **Lower threshold (e.g. 30)**: AI warns more often → catches more real failures, but also more false alarms
- **Higher threshold (e.g. 80)**: AI warns less often → fewer false alarms, but more failures slip through

The table below shows what the numbers would look like at every possible setting. Look at the **F1 %** column to find the threshold that gives the best overall balance for your team. The quality scores at the top of this page summarise the whole table as a single number.

panels.description: Each row shows what happens if you flag PRs as risky at a different sensitivity level. 'High only' is the most conservative — fewer warnings but more precise. 'Any risk (Low+)' catches the most failures but generates more false alarms. Use the F1 % column to find the best overall balance.

targets.rawSql:
WITH pr_data AS (
  SELECT risk_score AS max_risk_score, had_ci_failure AS ci_failed
  FROM ai_failure_predictions
  WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND prediction_outcome != ''
  AND ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}'
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
),
levels AS (
  SELECT 'High only'        AS label, 80 AS min_score, 1 AS sort_order UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Medium or higher',           50,              2               UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Any risk (Low+)',            10,              3
),
curve AS (
  SELECT l.label, l.sort_order,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= l.min_score AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score >= l.min_score AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  l.min_score AND pd.ci_failed = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fn,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pd.max_risk_score <  l.min_score AND pd.ci_failed = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS tn
  FROM levels l CROSS JOIN pr_data pd
  GROUP BY l.label, l.sort_order, l.min_score
)
SELECT
  label                                                                         AS `Flag as Risky When`,
  tp                                                                            AS `AI Warned → Failed (TP)`,
  fp                                                                            AS `AI Warned → Passed (FP)`,
  fn                                                                            AS `AI Missed → Failed (FN)`,
  tn                                                                            AS `AI OK → Passed (TN)`,
  ROUND(tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(tp + fp, 0), 1)                                    AS `Precision %`,
  ROUND(tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(tp + fn, 0), 1)                                    AS `Recall %`,
  ROUND(2 * tp * 100.0 / NULLIF(2 * tp + fp + fn, 0), 1)                      AS `F1 %`,
  ROUND(tn * 100.0 / NULLIF(tn + fp, 0), 1)                                    AS `Specificity %`
FROM curve
ORDER BY sort_order

panels.title: AI Performance at Different Sensitivity Levels

panels.description: AI-reviewed PRs that had no matching CI pipeline data and cannot be classified as TP/FP/FN/TN.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}' AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND prediction_outcome = 'NO_CI'

panels.title: PRs with No CI Data (?)

panels.description: Click a row to open the PR in GitHub. Use the Outcome filter above to focus on TP / FP / FN / TN / NO_CI. NO_CI rows are always shown regardless of the CI Source filter.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  pull_request_key AS `PR #`,
  pr_title AS `Title`,
  repo_name AS `Repository`,
  pr_author AS `Author`,
  MAX(risk_score) AS `Risk Score`,
  CASE WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS `AI Flagged`,
  CASE WHEN MAX(had_ci_failure) THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS `CI Failed`,
  CASE
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 1 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 1 THEN 'TP'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 1 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 0 AND ci_failure_source != 'none' THEN 'FP'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 0 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 1 THEN 'FN'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 0 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 0 AND ci_failure_source != 'none' THEN 'TN'
    ELSE 'NO_CI'
  END AS `Outcome`,
  ci_failure_source AS `CI Source`,
  GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ai_tool ORDER BY ai_tool SEPARATOR ', ') AS `AI Tools`,
  MAX(flagged_at) AS `Reviewed At`,
  CONCAT(MAX(additions), '+/', MAX(deletions), '-') AS `Size`,
  pr_url AS `URL`
FROM ai_failure_predictions
WHERE project_name = '${project}'
  AND repo_id IN (${repo_id:sqlstring})
  AND ai_tool IN (${ai_tool:sqlstring})
  AND $__timeFilter(flagged_at)
  AND (ci_failure_source = '${ci_failure_source}' OR prediction_outcome = 'NO_CI')
GROUP BY pull_request_id, pull_request_key, repo_name, pr_title, pr_author, pr_url, ci_failure_source
HAVING '${outcome_filter}' = 'All'
  OR (CASE
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 1 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 1 THEN 'TP'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 1 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 0 AND ci_failure_source != 'none' THEN 'FP'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 0 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 1 THEN 'FN'
    WHEN MAX(was_flagged_risky) = 0 AND MAX(had_ci_failure) = 0 AND ci_failure_source != 'none' THEN 'TN'
    ELSE 'NO_CI'
  END) = '${outcome_filter}'
ORDER BY `Reviewed At` DESC
LIMIT 500
